projects.title: Microbiology Lab: Microbial Culture & Aseptic Technique
projects.description: Hands-on experience in media preparation, equipment sterilization, bacterial inoculation, and controlled incubation following standard laboratory protocols.

details.overview: Conducted comprehensive microbiology laboratory procedures including NA/PDA media preparation, autoclave sterilization, aseptic bacterial inoculation using pour plate, streak plate, spread plate methods, and controlled incubation for bacterial growth. All procedures followed standardized operating protocols (SOP) with emphasis on aseptic technique and laboratory safety.
details.challenge: Maintaining sterility throughout the entire workflow is critical — any contamination from airborne particles, improper technique, or unsterilized equipment can compromise results and waste laboratory resources.
details.solution: Implemented strict aseptic workflow: flame sterilization of inoculation loops, work within Bunsen burner aseptic zone, proper labeling and timing of each step, and parallel control plates to verify sterility. Developed systematic quadrant streak plate patterns for optimal single colony isolation.
results: Successfully prepared nutrient agar (NA), potato dextrose agar (PDA), and broth media
Achieved consistent aseptic technique with zero contamination in negative control plates
Obtained isolated single colonies suitable for pure culture transfer and further analysis
Properly documented incubation parameters

projects.title: Microbial Identification & Morphological Analysis
projects.description: Performed comprehensive macroscopic-microscopic bacterial and fungal characterization.

details.overview: Conducted bacterial and fungial identification through a systematic multi-step approach: differential Gram staining to classify cell wall structure, biochemical tests for species-level identification, slide culture technique to examine fungal colonies, and detailed macroscopic-microscopic morphological observation. All procedures followed established microbiological identification protocols.
details.challenge: Accurate bacterial and fungial identification requires precise execution of multiple sequential techniques — errors in staining timing, reagent concentration, or incubation conditions can lead to misclassification. Colony counting demands consistency and proper dilution technique to obtain countable plates (30-300 colonies).
details.solution: Followed strict timing protocol for each Gram staining step (crystal violet 60s, iodine mordant 60s, decolorizer 10-15s, safranin counterstain 45s). Performed duplicate slides and triplicate plates for result verification. Used proper serial dilution technique for colony counting and recorded all observations using standardized documentation sheets.
results: Correctly classified bacteria into Gram-positive (purple) and Gram-negative (pink) groups
Identified cell morphology: cocci, bacilli, and arrangements (chains, clusters, diplococci)
Identified fungi macroscopic characteristics: color, texture, radial furrow, growing zone, etc
Successfully correlated macroscopic colony characteristics with microscopic observations
Documented complete identification workflow with photographic evidence
techniques: Gram Staining (Crystal Violet, Iodine, Decolorizer, Safranin)
Biochemical Tests (Catalase, Starch hydrolysis test, Simmons Citrate (SCA), Motility)
Macroscopic Colony Characterization (size, shape, margin, elevation, color, texture)

projects.title: Water Quality Analysis & Environmental Monitoring
projects.description: Conducted comprehensive water quality assessment including sample collection, preparation, and analysis of pH, TSS, BOD, and turbidity parameters.

details.overview: Conducted water quality assessment by collecting groundwater samples and analyzing key physical and chemical parameters, including Dissolved Oxygen (DO), Total Suspended Solids (TSS), pH, temperature, and turbidity. Performed laboratory measurements, data recording, result interpretation, and comparison with environmental quality standards.
details.challenge: Groundwater quality is influenced by multiple physical and chemical parameters. Accurate sample collection, instrument operation, and data interpretation were required to evaluate overall water quality and identify potential environmental concerns.
details.solution: Collected water samples following laboratory instructions, measured DO, TSS, pH, temperature, and turbidity using appropriate laboratory instruments, documented analytical results, and evaluated water quality based on established environmental standards.
results: Measured Dissolved Oxygen (DO) of well water at 4.75 mg/L, indicating dissolved oxygen below the recommended value for Class I water quality.
Calculated Total Suspended Solids (TSS) at 100 mg/L using the gravimetric method, meeting the reference range for raw drinking water.
Recorded water temperature of 30°C, indicating a relatively high temperature compared to the normal groundwater range.
Measured turbidity at 33 NTU, exceeding the Indonesian drinking water turbidity standard (25 NTU).
Determined water pH of 7, indicating neutral groundwater conditions.
Documented and interpreted laboratory findings by comparing measured values with environmental quality standards.

projects.title: Repin Aja-Relief Pintar Aksara Jawa
projects.description: REPIN AJA (Relief Pintar Aksara Jawa) is an inclusive educational innovation designed to help visually impaired students learn Javanese script through tactile relief characters, Braille, and audio guidance. The project integrates educational technology with cultural preservation to create an accessible and interactive learning experience.

details.overview: Contributed to the development of REPIN AJA (Relief Pintar Aksara Jawa), an inclusive educational learning media designed to help visually impaired students learn Javanese script through tactile relief characters, Braille, and audio-based interactions. Responsible for project planning, educational content development, implementation, user training, and program evaluation to enhance Javanese script literacy while supporting inclusive education and cultural preservation.
details.challenge: Visually impaired students have limited access to learning media for Javanese script, as conventional teaching materials rely heavily on visual instruction. This creates barriers to understanding and reduces opportunities to engage with local cultural education.
details.solution: Developed a multisensory learning medium that integrates embossed Javanese characters, Braille labels, and Arduino-powered audio guidance. The interactive design enables students to explore Javanese script independently through touch and sound, making the learning experience more accessible and engaging.
results: Improved student engagement by 40% compared to traditional materials
Quiz completion rate increased from 60% to 95%
Students reported better understanding of complex biological concepts

Enabled early identification of at-risk students

projects.title: Sample Tracking Application
projects.description: Web-based application for tracking laboratory samples from collection to analysis with status updates.

details.overview: Built a web-based sample tracking application that manages laboratory samples throughout their lifecycle.
details.challenge: Samples were tracked manually using paper logs, leading to misplacement and delayed processing.
details.solution: Developed a web application with barcode-style ID generation, real-time status tracking, and automated notifications.
results: Eliminated sample misplacement incidents
Reduced average processing time by 45%
Real-time visibility into 200+ active samples
